name: Example ICP — incumbent vertical SaaS
description: Edit to match your ICP. The model has three parts: (1) hard gates, each a boolean the account MUST pass — one failure rejects it regardless of score; (2) graded scoring dimensions, each worth up to max_points — the classify skill awards partial points with evidence; (3) tier thresholds on the normalized 0-100 score. priority_verticals are a hint for discover/classify, not a gate.

gates.description: Founded before the current AI-native wave — an incumbent with customers, not a brand-new startup.
Sells a product, not primarily services or consulting.
B2B or B2B2C — business customers, enterprise accounts, or partner channels.
Owns proprietary workflow/data (transactions, tickets, inventory, claims, schedules, documents, etc.).
Roughly 25-2000 employees, or smaller if clearly funded — enough budget to buy.
AI is not the founding premise or core category — leaves room for an AI gap to close.

dimensions.description: Distance between today's product and what AI could do for it — bigger gap, bigger opportunity.
Depth and defensibility of the proprietary data/workflow the product sits on.
Competitive or market pressure pushing them to act now (funding, churn, new entrants).
Ability to fund a deal and reachability of an economic buyer.
How tractable a first engagement looks — data accessible, scope bounded.
